2001-10-01.title: Dutch Frankness on Immigrants Treads Where Many Nations Fear to Go : Europe's Muslims:A Difficult Debate
2001-10-01.abstract: THE HAGUE—  With a six-column banner headline on page one, a leading Dutch newspaper reported last week that as a result of the terrorist attacks on the United States more than 60 percent of the Dutch agreed with the idea that Muslim immigrants who supported the attack should be expelled from the Netherlands.

2001-10-01.title: Support for U.S. Security Plans Is Quietly Voiced Across Canada
2001-10-01.abstract: In contrast to statement of Prime Min Jean Chretien, Canadians voice support of United States and say Canada should cooperate with US in areas of immigration, security and antiterrorism policies; photo (M)

2001-10-01.title: Gross! The Inside Story Of China's Festive Cake
2001-10-01.abstract: Moon cakes, elaborately decorated small round pastries that are traditional food of mid-autumn festival in China and were once considered delicacy, have in recent years become elaborately packaged seasonal food that is traded back and forth as gift, but rarely actually consumed in more than minute quantities; as range of food available to Chinese people expands, what was once considered rare and delectable treat is now generally avoided as too sugary and lacking freshness and flavor; photo (M)

2001-10-01.title: U.S. and Pakistan Discuss Nuclear Security
2001-10-01.abstract: American military and intelligence officials discuss with Pakistani government concerns over security of country's nuclear weapons stockpile and its two nuclear plants; discuss improving security and installing new safeguards amid fears that any attacks against terrorists in Afghanistan could boil over into Pakistan, where Afghan sympathizers in military could seize control of nuclear weapons; Pres Pervez Musharraf says nation's nuclear arsenal is safe and that there is no risk of weapons falling into wrong hands; formal limitations on how much assistance US can provide Pakistan because of latter's refusal to sign nonproliferation treaty discussed; US law imposing restrictions on sharing information about nuclear weapons with other countries noted (M)

2001-10-01.title: Blair Says He's Seen Proof of bin Laden Role
2001-10-01.abstract: British Prime Min Tony Blair says that he has seen incontrovertible evidence linking Osama bin Laden to Sept 11 attacks on America; statement is intended to offset doubt about whether such evidence exists; Gordon Brown, chancellor of exchequer, announces that Britain will take lead in freezing assets in campaign against bin Laden; photo (M)

2001-10-02.title: New Library of Alexandria Opens (Few) Doors
2001-10-02.abstract: Bibliotheca Alexandrina, modern incarnation of Library of Alexandria, stages temporary opening welcoming residents of once glamorous but now dilapidated port city in Egypt to use building for six weeks while administrators wrestle with long-delayed formal inauguration; director Ismail Serageldin says facility needs at least four additional months of construction work and continues to lack some basics expected of modern library, like Internet connections and computers (M)

2001-10-02.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Abrupt End To Royal Tempest
2001-10-02.abstract: britian: abrupt end to royal tempest*

2001-10-02.title: Arab-Israeli Cease-Fire? The Deaths Are Mounting, Along With the Finger-Pointing
2001-10-02.abstract: At least 19 Palestinians have died in week after Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon and Palestinian leader Yassir Arafat met to solidify terms of cease-fire; each side accuses other of not taking steps needed to make cease-fire work (M)

2001-10-02.title: A Sense of American Unfairness Erodes Support in Gulf States
2001-10-02.abstract: Sense of American unfairness toward Palestinians has eroded support for US fight against terrorism in prosperous, pro-Western Arab states in Persian Gulf where there is widespread revulsion for September 11 attacks and for methods of prime suspect, Osama bin Laden, but where feelings for Palestinians are at least as strong (M)

2001-10-02.title: World Briefing | Asia/Pacific: Indonesia: Irian Jaya Rebels On The Move

2001-10-03.title: NATO Says U.S. Has Proof Against bin Laden Group
2001-10-03.abstract: The alliance, finding Washington had "clear and compelling proof" that Osama bin Laden was behind the Sept. 11 attacks, invoked its mutual defense clause.

2001-10-03.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Raising Kursk, Racing Winter
2001-10-03.abstract: Divers rig steel cables to sunken Russian nuclear submarine Kursk in Barents Sea in preparation for raising it, but experts say Arctic winter will soon cut time for operation perilously short (S)

2001-10-03.title: World Briefing | Europe: The Hague: Indictments In Croatia Shelling
2001-10-03.abstract: Milan Zec, former chief of Yugoslav Navy, and three other former commanders--Miodrag Jokic, Pavle Strugar, Vladimir Kovacevic Zec--are indicted by war crimes tribunal in the Hague for causing unlawful civilian deaths while shelling Croatian port of Dubrovnik during Bosnian war in 1991; photo (S)

2001-10-03.title: Education Offers Women in Northern Afghanistan a Ray of Hope
2001-10-03.abstract: Islam practiced in rebel-held northern Afghanistan sets far less rigid restrictions on women than that enforced by Taliban government that controls most of nation; some of strict Muslim dictates also apply in rebel area, but girls are allowed to attend school, women can ride in back of trucks, can shop in market and can work at limited number of professions; photo (M)

2001-10-03.title: Pentagon Tries To Avoid Using Pakistan Bases
2001-10-03.abstract: Pentagon shapes war plan against terrorists to minimize use of Pakistani bases for strike against Osama bin Laden and Taliban in Afghanistan, fearing extensive deployments could destabilize crucial but volatile ally, one that has tested nuclear weapons; growing anti-American sentiment in region prompts Pentagon to scramble to make new arrangements; Defense Sec Donald Rumsfeld leaves for Saudi Arabia, Oman, Egypt and Uzbekistan to discuss using bases; new alliances are linking US to several authoritarian governments that have records of corruption and rights abuses but also fear radical Islamic movements; Rumsfeld points out importance of Uzbekistan's position bordering Afghanistan; US already trains Uzbeks in counterterrorism, drug interdiction and weapons smuggling; US now has more than 300 warplanes on ships and land bases from Indian Ocean to Red Sea; photo of USS Essex (M)

2001-10-04.title: Mideast Cease-Fire: Who Will Be the First to Scrap It?
2001-10-04.abstract: Mounting tit-for-tap violence continues to impede Pres Bush's efforts to secure long-term Isrsaeli-Palestinian peace; many believe only reluctance of each side to be first to end talks keeps Israelis and Palestinians from scrapping truce; Israeli tanks bombard Beit Lahia, Gaza Strip, while Israeli bulldozers ravage area farmland, in response to fatal attack on Israeli settlement of Alei Sinai; Palestinian snipers fire into crowd of thousands of Jewish worshippers, Hebron; map; photo (M)

2001-10-04.title: Rumsfeld Meets Saudis and Says He's Satisfied With Level of Support
2001-10-04.abstract: Defense Sec Donald Rumsfeld seeks to solidify Saudi support for strike against Osama bin Laden's terrorist network and broader war against terrorism; meets with King Fahd and other Saudi leaders, but insists he is not negotiating access to air bases, which is confirmed by Saudis; precise extent of cooperation is unclear; speculation on whether old disputes linger discussed; photo (M)

2001-10-04.title: DNA Map For Bacterium Of Plague Is Decoded
2001-10-04.abstract: Sanger Center biologists report decoding full DNA, or genome, of bacterium that causes plague; move is widely expected to lead to improved vaccines (M)

2001-10-04.title: World Briefing | Africa: South Africa: Top Lawmaker Arrested
2001-10-04.abstract: Tony Yengeni, chief whip in South Africa's Parliament for governing African National Congress, is arrested on corruption and fraud charges related to purchase of automobile at cut-rate price from defense contractor; denies wrongdoing (S)

2001-10-05.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Move To Ban Far-Right Party
2001-10-05.abstract: German supreme court says it will hear government motion to ban far-right National Democratic Party, which is accused of spreading racist ideology and inciting antiforeigner and anti-Semitic hate crimes (S)

2001-10-05.title: World Briefing | Asia/Pacific: Myanmar: Former Leader Failing
2001-10-05.abstract: Gen Ne Win, former strongman in Burma, is reported to be in very serious condition in hospital in Singapore (S)

2001-10-05.title: 76 on Board Perish As a Jet From Israel Explodes Off Russia
2001-10-05.abstract: Russian airliner carrying 76 people traveling from Tel Aviv to Siberia explodes and plunges into Black Sea off Russian coast; all 64 passengers and 12 crew members of Siberian Airlines jet are presumed dead; Pres Vladimir V Putin, who has thrown Russian support behind United States campaign against terrorism, says disaster may have been work of terrorists, conclusion quickly echoed by Russian intelligence officials; but there are suggesstions from American officials that crash could have been caused by antiaircraft missiles fired by Ukrainian military forces who were conducting training exercises, with Russian help, off Crimean peninsula; Ukrainian Defense Ministry adamantly denies any role in crash, saying that all its missiles hit their intended targets and that exercises were staged far from jet's flight path; map; photo (M)

2001-10-05.title: Fearing Unrest, China Presses Muslim Group
2001-10-05.abstract: China is cracking down on Uighurs in major effort to suppress potential unrest among the Muslim Turkic ethnic group, which poses most credible threat of any group to country's internal cohesion; China has joined America's developing alliance against Islamic terrorism in part because it is eager to win international support for its own campaign against Uighur separatists, who have long been viewed with sympathy in West; separatists claim that government policies are marginalizing country's eight million Uighurs and will eventually lead to their cultural annihilation; some Uighurs from Xinjiang Province are known to have fought along side of Afghanistan's Taliban in preparation, they say, for fighting for their cause in China; map; photo (M)

2001-10-05.title: Sharon Invokes Munich in Warning U.S. on 'Appeasement'
2001-10-05.abstract: Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon warns United States that it risks appeasing Arab nations in way European democracies appeased Hitler on eve of World War II; unusually harsh and public rebuke reflects rising frustration over Bush administration's approach to battling terrorism; remarks come after Russian airliner enroute from Tel Aviv explodes over Black Sea and Palestinian terrorist attack on bus station in northern Israel (M)

2001-10-06.title: World Briefing | The Americas: Canada: Ban On Same-Sex Marriage
2001-10-06.abstract: Argentine Pres Fernando de la Rua and other officials publicly insist they will not devalue peso, despite fally revenues and stock prices and soaring interest rates (S)

2001-10-06.title: Dr. Rhys Jones, 60, Researcher Of Human Origins in Australia
2001-10-06.abstract: Dr Rhys Maengwyn Jones, pioneer investigator of human origins in Australia, whose research pushed back beginning of human settlement there to far earlier date than previously thought, dies at age 60; photo (M)

2001-10-06.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Space Tourist Is Rejected
2001-10-06.abstract: Russian space agency turns down potential space tourist, South African tycoon Mark Shuttleworth, for being too demanding (S)

2001-10-06.title: World Briefing | Asia/Pacific: The Koreas: Talks On A Road Link Stall
2001-10-06.abstract: Two Koreas fail to agree on land route linking South Korea to Mount Kumkang, just above demilitarized zone (S)

2001-10-06.title: TALIBAN'S RIVALS SHOW OFF FORCES
2001-10-06.abstract: Northern Alliance in Afghanistan shows off 40 Russian-built tanks in military parade near Tajik border, as leaders suggest offensive against Taliban could come in form of powerful strike toward Kabul coordinated with US air strikes; photos; map; equipment appears new, in contrast to broken-down weaponry seen in other alliance areas; soldiers have new fatigues, Kalashnikov rifles and rocket launchers; speculation noted about military advance (M)

2001-10-07.title: U.S. and Britain Clearing Way for 'Relentless' Campaign, Bush Says
2001-10-07.abstract: The United States, supported by Britain, today launched a long-anticipated attack on Afghanistan, dropping bombs and firing devastating volleys of cruise missiles.

2001-10-07.title: Ukrainians Shift Stance On the Cause Of Air Crash
2001-10-07.abstract: Investigators in Sochi, Russia, study wreckage of Siberian Airlines jet that crashed into Black Sea as Pres Putin reportedly rejects Ukraine's denial that plane was shot down by accident; Russian Min Sergei B Ivanov caustically refers to 'so-called version' of disaster that killed 78 people and says Putin wants details of Ukraine's launch of antiaircraft missile four minutes before jet disappeared (M)

2001-10-07.title: On Video, bin Laden Lashes Out at U.S.
2001-10-07.abstract: Osama bin Laden was shown on television this afternoon, declaring that "America is full of fear" and will never be safe and secure until Palestinians feel safe and secure.

2001-10-07.title: G-7 Leaders Pledge To Work In Concert to Thwart Terrorists
2001-10-07.abstract: World's leading industrial nations agree to share intelligence information relating to financing of terrorism and to pressure other countries to prevent terrorists from using global banking system (M)

2001-10-07.title: Thunderous Attack Heard in Kabul
2001-10-07.abstract: Sending bright flashes of light and loud concussions rolling across the farming plain just 35 miles north of Kabul, American military forces began what looked like a steady bombardment.

2001-10-08.title: Iranian Security Forces Trying to Block Fleeing Afghans at the Porous Border
2001-10-08.abstract: Iran tries to enforce closed border with Afghanistan and many Afghans seeking refuge there will be quickly deported; Iran is already host to 1.4 million Afghan refugees; photo (M)

2001-10-08.title: Dougie Millings, 88, the Tailor for the Beatles
2001-10-08.abstract: Dougie Millings, tailor to stars whose crowning achievement was outfitting the Beatles, dies at age 88 (S)

2001-10-08.title: U.S. AND BRITAIN STRIKE AFGHANISTAN, AIMING AT BASES AND TERRORIST CAMPS; BUSH WARNS 'TALIBAN WILL PAY A PRICE'
2001-10-08.abstract: US and Britain launch powerful barrage of cruise missiles and long-range bombers against Afghanistan on October 8 in campaign to destroy terrorist training camps of Osama bin Laden's Al Qaeda network and Taliban government that has protected it; Pres Bush announces start of military campaign in nationwide televised address; says 'carefully targeted' actions are designed to disrupt use of Afghanistan as terrorist base of operations and to attack military capability of bin Laden organization; says Taliban failed to meet demands to surrender bin Laden, stop supporting terrorism and release foreign aid workers they hold; reiterates warning to nations that sponsor or protect terrorists; address is followed by statement of support from British Prime Min Tony Blair; both leaders emphasize that military campaign is not war against Islam; bin Laden issues own threat in videotape broadcast worldwide; stakes claim to lead all Muslims in fight against America, which he casts as murderer, with Israel, of Iraqis and Palestinians; praises hijackers who staged September 11 attacks on US; vows America will not live in peace until peace reigns in Palestine; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld says bin Laden is not specific target; says initial bombing is intended to create conditions for sustained anti-terrorist operations against his organization; says US is seeking to orchestrate overthrow of Taliban by providing military aid and developing relationships with groups that oppose regime and terrorists they support; reiterates that US is not at war with Islam, noting armed forces have been sent abroad five times in last decade to defend oppressed Muslim populations; administration officials indicate opening aerial campaign can go on for week and will be followed by ground operations by Special Forces units to garner information and hunt down leaders of Al Qaeda network; initial bombardment described; is carried out by 15 land-based bombers and 25 strike aircraft from aircraft carriers; weapons include precision guided bombs; American warships and British submarines fire 50 Tomahawk cruise missiles; witnesses say skies over capital of Kabul are lit up with flashes; say thunderous explosions are heard throughout night; report attacks around cities of Kandahar and Jalalabad, strongholds of radical Islamic regime; Pentagon sends C-17 cargo planes to drop relief supplies of food and medicine for hundreds of thousands of refugees believed fleeing Afghan cities during bombing campaign; photos (M)

2001-10-08.title: European Leaders Voice Support
2001-10-08.abstract: US-led attacks on Afghanistan garner support from European leaders, including French Pres Jacques Chirac, German Chancellor Gerhard Schroder, Italian Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi and Belgian Prime Min Guy Verhofstadt, as well as other allies throughout world; Russia issues statement of support; photos (M)

2001-10-08.title: Quietly, Carefully, President Worked Toward a Decision on Attack-Aid Combination
2001-10-08.abstract: Article discusses Pres Bush's decision to begin military campaign against Taliban government of Afghanistan, which includes drops of food, medicine and transistor radios that would let Afghan people receive Washington's message (M)

2001-10-09.title: Copter Downed In Caucasus; 5 From U.N. Among 9 Dead
2001-10-09.abstract: Five UN observers and four other people die after rebels shoot down helicopter in Abkhazia, separatist region of western Georgia on Black Sea (S)

2001-10-09.title: A Wary Warship Launches New Salvos
2001-10-09.abstract: Senior officer aboard USS Enterprise in Arabian Sea discusses war so far, saying greatest worry is potential for retaliatory action, expressing sense that Sept 11 terrorist attacks have changed traditional rules of war; fighter pilots encountered limited, unguided antiaircraft artillery on initial strikes in Afghanistan; officer describes beginning of next wave of strikes, as fighter planes and support aircraft thunder into action; photo (M)

2001-10-09.title: A Greek Revival:A New Take on Classicism
2001-10-09.abstract: ATHENS—  With cascading drapes one of this summer's top fashion stories, Greek chic has never looked so cool. So how does an eternal ideal become a hot trend?

2001-10-09.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Power To The Provinces
2001-10-09.abstract: Italian voters approve referendum endorsing constitutional changes to increase regional autonomy at expense of central government's power (S)

2001-10-09.title: World Briefing | The Americas: Colombia: Lawmaker Slain
2001-10-09.abstract: Rep Luis Alfredo Colmenares is assassinated, Bogota; is second congressman from Colombia's war-torn Arauca province killed in week (S)

2001-10-10.title: Pakistani Police Kill 3 Protesters and a 13-Year-Old
2001-10-10.abstract: Three anti-American protesters and 13-year-old boy are shot dead by Pakistani police officers as demonstrators try to storm police station in Kuchlak, town near Afghan border; deaths come on second day of violent protests against American-led airstrikes on Afghanistan; longer air attacks on Afghanistan continue, greater danger that protests could threaten stability of Pakistan; public opinion poll says 62 percent of Pakistanis oppose involvement in war; photo; map; chart (M)

2001-10-10.title: World Briefing | Asia: Indonesia: Suharto Kin's Trial Opens
2001-10-10.abstract: Ari Sigit, grandson of former Pres Suharto of Indonesia, goes on trial on weapons charges (S)

2001-10-10.title: Bin Laden Aide Threatens More Strikes
2001-10-10.abstract: Sleiman Abou-Gheith, top aide to Osama bin Laden, appears in videotaped message and implies that airplane hijackings like those that killed thousands of Americans at World Trade Center and Pentagon could be repeated; says hijackers who died in attacks did 'a good deed'; tape, broadcast by Al Jazeera television network, is directed explicitly at world's one billion Muslims, and takes form of appeal for worldwide uprising against United States and against Americans; Abou-Gheith uses bombing of Afghanistan as rallying point for Muslims willing to die for their faith; photo (M)

2001-10-10.title: Anti-American Protests Increase, and Sponsors Plan More
2001-10-10.abstract: Anti-American demonstrations widen in Indonesia, with riot police firing tear gas to disperse angry protesters outside United States Embassy in Jakarta; radical Muslim groups say they are preparing larger demonstrations and threaten to attack foreigners in Indonesia; about 10,000 Americans live in Indonesia; Pres Megawati Sukarnoputri has announced qualified support for US in its campaign against terrorism in wake of Sept 11 attacks, but anger of fringe groups holding demonstrations reflects broad resentment of US and opposition to its bombing of Afghanistan (M)

2001-10-10.title: Winning Party In Poland Forges Link With Farmers
2001-10-10.abstract: Poland's former Communist Party, which came out ahead in national elections last month, forms coalition with Polish Peasants Party; will now be able to form government, but may have to pay homage to nationalistic and anti-European factions (M)

2001-10-11.title: World Briefing | Africa: Congo: Rebels Set Peace Strategy
2001-10-11.abstract: Rebel groups in Congo meet with Rwandan and Ugandan backers to prepare for peace talks in Ethiopia (S)

2001-10-11.title: 56 Islamic Nations Avoid Condemning U.S. Attacks, but Warn on Civilian Casualties
2001-10-11.abstract: Fifty-six Islamic nations holding emergency meeting in Doha, Qatar, issue statement that does not directly condemn Sept 11 attacks on America, but warns against inflicting civilian casualties or striking against other Arab states; careful wording reflects complex pressures and fears that weigh on Arab and Muslim governments; photo (M)

2001-10-11.title: World Briefing | The Americas: Mexico: Fox's Party Loses
2001-10-11.abstract: Mexican federal tribunal, citing evidence of election violations, overturns National Action Party's mayoral victory in Ciudad Juarez in political setback for party of Pres Vicente Fox; rules Mayor Gustavo Elizondo unfairly used office to influence outcome (S)

2001-10-11.title: U.S. Missions Are Stocking Antibiotics
2001-10-11.abstract: State Dept orders diplomatic missions to stock up on antibiotics to counter any attack using anthrax (M)

2001-10-12.title: Plumbing the Mysteries of Inheritance
2001-10-12.abstract: Sampling of work by V S Naipaul, winner of Nobel Prize in Literature (M)

2001-10-12.title: Afghan Region's Politics Snarl U.S. War Plan
2001-10-12.abstract: Goals set by Pres Bush to root out Al Qaeda terrorists and Afghanistan's Taliban government become deeply entangled in murky politics that have kept Afghans fighting for more than 20 years; decision not to bomb Taliban front lines at point where breakdown would leave Kabul open to immediate capture by opposition Northern Alliance reflects grim strategic reality: Pakistan, crucial ally for US in meeting Bush's war aims, has threatened to close its airspace to American aircraft and cancel support if bombing allows Northern Alliance to overrun Kabul; Pakistan regards Northern Alliance, which has been supported by Iran and Russia, with deep suspicion; Gen Pervez Musharraf, Pakistan's president, claims he has ironclad understanding with Washington that Northern Alliance will not be allowed to 'draw mileage' from American bombing by closing in on Kabul before arrangements are complete for new government that will be friendly to Pakistan, as Taliban has been; as result, US military strategy could become hostage to bitter enmities that have long bedeviled Afghan politics, allowing Taliban to consolidate its position; photos (M)

2001-10-12.title: LONG WAR IS SEEN
2001-10-12.abstract: United States and Britain make clear that engagement in Afghanistan might stretch into next summer or beyond; Pres Bush, in news conference, says military operation will continue for days, months or even years; Adm Sir Michael Boyce, Britain's top commander, says that if ground forces are called for, commando brigade of Royal Marines now on exercises in Oman would most likely be used; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld is circumspect when asked if US might use significant number of ground troops inside Afghanistan, but says preparations are in progress for using such troops; American and British officials outline overall strategy of destroying Afghanistan's command and control structure from air, thereby clearing way for military operations by specialty units and for covert operations already under way; option of pursuing other terrorists groups in other Arab countries is still to be explored; American officials are concerned that allies such as Egypt and Saudi Arabia do not yet fully grasp that Bush's 'new war' against terrorism may not end with elimination of Osama bin Laden and could involve chasing terrorist groups in other countries; photo (M)

2001-10-12.title: Frankfurt Journal; Can't Say That? Muzzling the Modern Germans
2001-10-12.abstract: Article on new limits of acceptable speech in Germany as country's leaders eagerly support United States in its battle against terrorism; popular television anchorman comes close to losing his job after suggesting on air that Pres Bush and Osama bin Laden have both displayed intolerance; two schoolteachers have been suspended for making comments that seemed sympathetic to terrorists who attacked US; community access cable television channel blocks call-in show that local Iranian resident has conducted for fear that callers might voice religious hatred or promote violence; some Germans feel uneasy to see their support of US leading to new taboos to conform with fight against terrorism; photos (M)

2001-10-12.title: Flow of Afghan Refugees Is Stalled by Pakistan
2001-10-12.abstract: United Nations refugee agency head Ruud Lubbers, in unusually blunt statement, says Pakistani security concerns and curbs have stalled preparations for expected influx of Afghan refugees; says support has been weak from governments in region and internationally; expected refugee flood has not yet appeared, in part because American bombing has not stirred panic and because all six neighbors have closed borders; photos (M)

2001-10-13.title: North Korea Cancels Reunions With the South
2001-10-13.abstract: North Korea cancels fourth set of family reunions with Koreans in South (S)

2001-10-13.title: A NATION CHALLENGED: ISLAM -- Nairobi, Kenya; Thousands Hear Call Of Prayer and Politics At World's Mosques
2001-10-13.abstract: Several hundred Muslims pour out of Jamia Mosque in Nairobi, Kenya, and begin denouncing US; Sheik Haroum Odundo says every single Muslim is being targed by United States and and every Muslim is Osama bin Laden (S)

2001-10-13.title: Thousands Hear Call of Prayer and Politics at World's Mosques
2001-10-13.abstract: In mosques yesterday, Muslims gathered for Friday Prayers, and in many instances the preaching was political and sharply anti-American.

2001-10-13.title: Samarkand Journal; Tamerlane's Capital Suffers From Lack of Tourists
2001-10-13.abstract: Samarkand, legendary jewel astride ancient Silk Route, has lost its tourists to fears of war to its south in Afghanistan; photos; map; only people coming into Uzbekistan now are American soldiers pouring in to prepare for military action (Samarkand Journal) (M)

2001-10-14.title: The 22 Most Wanted Suspects, in a Five-Act Drama of Global Terror
2001-10-14.abstract: Brief descriptions of 22 men on United States list of most wanted terrorists in world; photos (L)

2001-10-14.title: Flee to a Camp. To What Kind of Life?
2001-10-14.abstract: Nearly one million Afghan refugees live in dozens of camps in or near Peshawar, in the Northwest Frontier Province of Pakistan.

2001-10-14.title: A Defense From Portugal For the Noble Wine Cork
2001-10-14.abstract: Synthetic wine bottle corks pose threat to Portugal's natural cork industry, which supplies 85 percent of all wine corks; Portuguese have scrambled to create new breed of cork able to compete with taint-free, consistent performance offered by synthetics; photo (M)

2001-10-14.title: Under Noisy Skies, Britons Assert a Right to Sleep
2001-10-14.abstract: British government faces European Court of Human Rights ruling that could aid residents of Brentford, Eng, which is directly under Heathrow Airport's noisy flight paths, and have implications across Europe; court found Britian violates European Convention on Human Rights by failing to strike fair balance between nation's needs and people's right to good night's sleep; Britain, at least, must rethink process by which it decides how often night flights land at airport and may have to ground such flights; map; photos (M)

2001-10-14.title: Signs of Change In Terror Goals Went Unheeded
2001-10-14.abstract: Intelligence officials now say that cryptic message intercepted by Central Intelligence Agency last year from Al Qaeda member boasting that Osama bin Laden was planning 'Hiroshima' against America was telling sign of drastic shift in last three years in Al Qaeda's ambitions and global reach; say United States failed to grasp group's transformation from obscure group of Islamic extremists into world's most dangerous terrorists; key factor in shift was bin Laden's decision to act as franchiser of terrorism, providing financial and logistical aid to locally sponsored plots brought to his group by Islamic extremists; extent of group's US operations has stunned Federal Bureau of Investigation; chart; photos (M)

2001-10-15.title: Without Charismatic General, Rebels Seem to be Trying to Find Their Way
2001-10-15.abstract: Recent assassination of Northern Alliance leader Gen Ahmed Shah Massoud is slowing alliance's diplomatic and military efforts; his loss is being felt most deeply in efforts to persuade Taliban commanders to defect to alliance; alliance has yet to mount major military attack since start of United States airstrikes, even in areas where jets have bombed front-line Taliban positions; photo (M)

2001-10-15.title: Afghan Family Salvages Little but Its Hopes
2001-10-15.abstract: Interview with Afzal, Afghan father of four who has lived through long, painful arc of Afghan history and is testament to country's present plight; like most Afghans he is illiterate, but remains determined and resourceful survivor after 23 years of invasion and civil war; is optimistic country's hour may have finally come with American military campaign to dislodge ruling Taliban, and is optimistic that better era than his own awaits his children; photo (M)

2001-10-15.title: Irish Rebury 10 Republicans Hanged by British in 1920's
2001-10-15.abstract: Ireland buries remains of 10 Irish Republican Army volunteers who were executed by British during country's war of independence 80 years ago, with display of both solemnity and pomp; state funeral, only 13th in country's history since independence, brings much of Dublin to standstill for more than six hours as thousands line streets; photo (M)

2001-10-15.title: Fast Track, Slow Track
2001-10-15.abstract: Military analysis of weeklong US air strikes on Afghanistan; widespread and intensive military campaign is outstripping diplomatic maneuvers as behind-the-scenes effort to organize leadership that would replace Taliban leaders has made no discernible progress; map; photo (M)

2001-10-15.title: Reykjavik Journal; Whaling Ban, Not NATO, Heats Iceland's Temper
2001-10-15.abstract: Focus on debate over resumption whaling in Iceland, which resigned from International Whaling Commission in 1991 and forfeited right to catch up to 400 whales annually for 'scientific research;' despite opposition by whale lovers, poll suggests 75 percent of population backs resumption of whaling; maps; photos (M)

2001-10-16.title: Northern Alliance Casts Doubt on Taliban Participation in Next Government
2001-10-16.abstract: A senior official of the Northern Alliance declared today that his group would not accept any members of the Taliban in a future coalition government set up to run the country once the current Taliban leadership is driven from power.

2001-10-16.title: Taliban Envoy Talks of a Deal Over bin Laden
2001-10-16.abstract: Taliban Foreign Min Wakil Ahmed Muttawakil, in secret talks in Pakistan, appeals for American bombing pause in Afghanistan, while moderates in Taliban government seek to persuade supreme leader Mullah Muhammad Omar to agree to formula for handover of Osama bin Laden; talks come amid widespread rumors of Muttawakil's defection; his presence in Pakistan, apparently without Omar's approval, suggests intensive American bombing is causing policy shift in Taliban leadership; plea for bombing pause reportedly relayed to Sec Colin Powell, who is on one-day visit; Pakistani officials are optimistic Powell's visit will compress political process, accelerating steps to form post-Taliban government and clear way for American military commanders to pound Taliban front-line troops into disarray, defection or disintegration; photos (M)

2001-10-16.title: La Courneuve Journal; In Suburban Squalor Near Paris, Echoes of Jihad
2001-10-16.abstract: Tensions between France's Muslims, most from North Africa, and rest of French society bubbles into open, with echoes of jihad resounding in sprawling working-class suburbs that surround many French cities; squalid slums are home to mostly poor Muslims, caught up in identity crisis and suffer sting of racism and plague of unemployment; conditions in La Cite des 4,000, slum in La Courneuve suburb of Paris where many regard Osama bin Laden as savior, discussed; map; photos (M)

2001-10-16.title: Koreans Protest as Japanese Leader Arrives to Express Contrition
2001-10-16.abstract: Prime Min Junichiro Koizumi of Japan visits Seoul, South Korea, to express contrition for country's brutal colonial past and is greeted by energetic protests from civilians and from opposition politicians, who accuse him of lacking sincerity in dealing with historical issues; is forced to cancel address to national legislature after outpouring of objections from opposition parties (M)

2001-10-16.title: Special Operations Gunship Being Used Against Taliban
2001-10-16.abstract: Pentagon acknowledges dispatch of AC-130 gunship operated by special operations forces into skies over Afghanistan; use of lumbering AC-130 seen as indication of increasing American confidence that threat from Taliban air defense is greatly diminished; Pentagon officials say bombing campaign, originally expected to last week, has been spurred on longer by discovery of Taliban troops on move, and by identification of new targets from fresh intelligence (M)

2001-10-17.title: Finding the Presence of Anthrax
2001-10-17.abstract: Sources: Dr. Stuart Isaacs and Dr. Elizabeth Colston, both of the University of Pennsylvania

2001-10-17.title: Peace Talks To End War In Congo Finally Begin
2001-10-17.abstract: Long-awaited Congo peace talks open in Addis Ababa, amid doubts about its ability to end war (S)

2001-10-17.title: World Briefing | Asia: Philippines: The People V. Mrs. Marcos
2001-10-17.abstract: Imelda Marcos, former first lady of Philippines, is released on bail after court ordered her arrest on four counts of corruption involving transactions of more than $350 million in money she is accused of stealing in 1970's (S)

2001-10-17.title: Venezuela Waits for 'Revolution' to Bear Fruit
2001-10-17.abstract: Pres Hugo Chavez's approval rating falls from 75 to 56 percent as Venezuelans wait for his reforms to materialize; photo; left-leaning president, elected in landslide in 1998, redrafted Constitution, took control of Assembly and used oil revenues to steer through period of lowered inflation and increased foreign reserves; government has tried to extricate country from recession by raising spending but many experts and former supporters see few real gains for vast majority (M)

2001-10-17.title: Rumsfeld to Appeal to Arab Public on Mideast TV Network
2001-10-17.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld speaks on Middle East television network, Al Jazeera, in Qatar, to assure Arab viewers that United States is waging war against terrorists in Afghanistan, not Afghan people; Rumsfeld becomes latest Bush administration official to grant interview to Al Jazeera, which has angered American officials by broadcasting tapes made by Osama bin Laden (S)

2001-10-18.title: Bush Says Aim Is to Ease Entry Of Land Force
2001-10-18.abstract: Pres Bush says American air attacks in Afghanistan are aimed at paving way for friendly anti-Taliban forces on ground to defeat government; says enemy's air force and air defenses are being demolished; aide says Bush's reference was to Northern Alliance and anti-Taliban groups in Afghanistan, and not to use of American and British troops; AC-130 gunships flown by special operations forces blast target around Kandahar, important Taliban stronghold; warplanes employed over no-flight zones in southern Iraq join in aerial bombardment for first time; military official says majority of attacks are focused on military garrisons, airfields and air defenses especially around Kandahar and Kabul; attacks are far from front lines being patrolled by Northern Alliance, whose representatives are complaining that US is not doing enough to help; spokesman for Joint Chiefs of Staff says US military is making no special effort to coordinate strategy with anti-Taliban group, but is systematically attacking elements of Taliban military that will eliminate its ability to support Al Qaeda terrorist network; photo (M)

2001-10-18.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ukraine: Suspensions Over Missile Error
2001-10-18.abstract: Ukrainian Defense Min Oleksandr Kuzmuk suspends commanders of air defense force and of air defense training, first disciplinary moves since military accidentally downed Russian passenger plane (S)

2001-10-18.title: Powell's Message: America's Courting of Pakistan Will Not Come at India's Expense
2001-10-18.abstract: Bush administration moves to reassure India that America's intensifying alliance with rival Pakistan will not come at its expense; visiting Sec of State Colin L Powell tells Indian leaders US is concerned about Pakistani-inspired terrorism in Kashmir and equates September 11 attacks on US with October 1 terrorist assault in Srinagar, Kashmir capital, that killed more than 40 civilians; renews Washington's offer to get more involved in resolving festering conflict; meets with Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee, who says India is determined to come to terms with Pakistan, despite problems over Kashmir; photo (M)

2001-10-18.title: World Briefing | Asia: Philippines: Rebels Kidnap Italian Priest
2001-10-18.abstract: Eight men kidnap Italian priest, Rev Giuseppe Pierantoni, while he says Mass in church in southern Philippines; flee with him in boat; incident is close to area where Abu Sayyaf guerrillas hold 10 hostages (S)

2001-10-18.title: Bush's New Focus Requires A Shift in His China Policy
2001-10-18.abstract: Pres Bush's trip to Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation Forum in Shanghai, China, is expected to result in significant shift in his policy toward Beijing as he seeks to build, maintain and expand global coalition against terrorism in response to September 11 attacks; stresses focus will not be on trade issues that traditionally dominate Asian gathering; attacks force shift by administration to develop strategic partnership with China, which president formerly disdained; aides scramble to find middle ground at summit, with declaration against terrorism that leaves every country to define how it pursues mission; other planned highlights of Bush's trip discussed (M)

2001-10-19.title: World Briefing | Asia: Philippines: Execution Moratorium Lifted
2001-10-19.abstract: Filipino Pres Gloria Macapagal Arroyo lifts moratorium on capital punishment and says kidnappers should be executed as soon as possible; ransom kidnappings are entrenched in Philippines (S)

2001-10-19.title: Anti-Western and Extremist Views Pervade Saudi Schools
2001-10-19.abstract: The textbook for one of the five religion classes required of all 10th graders in Saudi public high schools tackles the complicated issue of who good Muslims should befriend.    After examining a number of scriptures which warn of the dangers of having Christian and Jewish friends, the lesson concludes: ''It is compulsory for the Muslims to be loyal to each other and to consider the infidels their enemy.''

2001-10-19.title: World Briefing | Asia: Singapore: Early Elections
2001-10-19.abstract: Singapore government dissolves legislature and sets election, reportedly anticipating economic downturn that could cost it support (S)

2001-10-19.title: Zhang Xueliang, 100, Dies; Warlord and Hero of China
2001-10-19.abstract: Zhang Xueliang, onetime warlord who in two turbulent weeks in 1936 helped turn course of Chinese history -- and then spent next 55 years under house arrest, gradually and reluctantly becoming national hero -- dies at age 100; photo (M)

2001-10-19.title: World Briefing | Asia: Cambodia: 32 On Trial Over Rebellion
2001-10-19.abstract: Twenty-eight suspects go on trial in Cambodia, accused of trying to overthrow government last November; 32 other defendants were convicted in earlier trial (S)

2001-10-20.title: Frustrating U.S., China Balks At Pact to Stem Missile Sales
2001-10-20.abstract: Bush administration is frustrated by China's resistance to implementing its Nov 2000 accord not to sell missile technology to nations developing nuclear arms despite United States pressure in advance of summit meeting between Pres Bush and Pres Jiang Zemin; wants China to supply list of missile parts and missile-making technology that it would ban from export and show how it would enforce ban (M)

2001-10-20.title: World Briefing
2001-10-20.abstract: THE AMERICAS NICARAGUA: PANEL TO HEAR ORTEGA CASE The Inter-American Commission on Human Rights has agreed to hear a case stemming from unresolved sexual-abuse allegations by Zoilamerica Narvaez Murillo, a stepdaughter of Daniel Ortega, the former Sandinista leader. She said in 1998 that she had been abused by Mr. Ortega from age 11, but the case stalled when courts refused to lift his immunity as a congressman. The commission will decide only whether the government failed to provide adequate remedies.    David Gonzalez (NYT).

2001-10-20.title: World Briefing | The Americas: Canada: Meeting Shifts From India
2001-10-20.abstract: Ottawa scrambles to prepare for mid-November meeting of bankers and finance ministers from 20 leading industrial and developing nations, after India backs out of holding event, citing fears of terrorism (S)

2001-10-20.title: Rome Journal; A Bush Admirer Longs to Join America's A-List
2001-10-20.abstract: Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi of Italy seems thwarted in his efforts to forge close relationship with Pres Bush; one reason is Berlusconi's recent comments about superiority of Western civilization over Islam, another is fact that Italy, with its revolving-door governments, has difficulty being taken with seriousness and respect it feels it deserves; photo (M)

2001-10-20.title: World Briefing | Asia: Sri Lanka: Journalists Seek Investigation
2001-10-20.abstract: More than 200 journalists sign letter to Pres Chandrika Kumaratunga of Sr Lanka demanding more active investigation of assassination year ago of Mylvaganam Nimalarajan, who reported for BBCV's Tamil and Sinhala langeuge services from Jaffna Peninsula; rights workers suspect Nimalarajan was killed because of his reporting on vote rigging in last year's election (S)

2001-10-21.title: Brazil Searches for More Energy
2001-10-21.abstract: Brazil's government planners hope to harness Amazon basin's network of rivers to remedy severe energy shortages in urban areas far to south; photo; map; work is speeded up on world's third-largest dam on bend in Xingu River east of Altamira, which would increase country's hydroelectric capacity fifteen percent; photo; map; ambitious programs have so far generated only fierce opposition among local environmental and religious groups (M)

2001-10-21.title: A NATION CHALLENGED: AN OVERVIEW: Oct. 20, 2001; War on the Ground, Allies Around the Globe, F.B.I. on the Case
2001-10-21.abstract: Expect more quick forays into Afghanistan by American special forces. That was the message from Pentagon officials after the assault Friday night by more than 100 Army Rangers and other elite forces, the first major ground operation in the war against Osama bin Laden's terrorist network and its Taliban protectors.    RISK IS CERTAIN -- Mindful of the Soviet Union's sorry experience in Afghanistan, a decade of quagmire and the loss of 15,000 troops, American officials said they planned to rely on lightning raids by commando teams, operations expected to be short but intense. The aim, they said, is to surprise and outpace the enemy. But that also involves close combat and inevitable risk. American casualties are more than likely, it was suggested.

2001-10-21.title: Israel Debates the Cost of Dealing With Arafat
2001-10-21.abstract: News analysis on debate within Sharon government in Israel over whether to begin international campaign to undermine Yasir Arafat's legitimacy as leader of Palestinians; photo (M)

2001-10-21.title: First of the Stinging Jabs
2001-10-21.abstract: Military analysis of nighttime commando raid in Afghanistan as opening new fast-paced stage of war against terrorism, first of series of stinging jabs intended to unhinge enemy; photo; Pentagon has warned that this phase may be drawn out and will not end until Qaeda network is destroyed and Taliban is toppled, but individual operations will be short and intense, at least for now; US military intends to show it has learned from 1993, when Somalis accustomed to US tactics shot down Black Hawk helicopter and killed 18 Americans (M)

2001-10-21.title: Moderate Muslims Fear Their Message Is Being Ignored
2001-10-21.abstract: Since the September  attacks, Al Azhar  the revered mosque, the distinguished university, the leading voice of the Sunni Muslim establishment -- has renewed with accustomed grace the roles it has played in the world of Islam for more than 1,000 years.    It has sought to advise Muslims around the world that those who kill in the name of Islam are nothing more than heretics. It has sought to guide, to reassure Westerners against any clash of civilizations.

2001-10-22.title: Bush and Putin: Accord on Terrorism
2001-10-22.abstract: Following are excerpts from the joint news conference in Shanghai yesterday by President Bush and President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia, as provided by the White House:    PRESIDENT PUTIN      The consequences of the tragic events of Sept. 11 -- political, economic, psychological consequences -- today are acutely felt in many countries and in all the continents. I think nobody has doubts the unprecedented carnage of the terrorists requires our united efforts, unification of the efforts of international community to fight terrorism. And we prepared and we issued a joint statement in this regard.

2001-10-22.title: Edward Radford, 79, Scholar Of the Risks From Radiation
2001-10-22.abstract: Dr Edward P Radford, who energetically promoted a higher estimate of cancer risk from radiation exposure and whose position was eventually upheld, dies at age 79; photo (M)

2001-10-22.title: Bush and Putin Declare They Can Alter ABM Pact
2001-10-22.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia and President Bush said they could reach agreements that would alter the 1972 Antiballistic Missile Treaty.

2001-10-22.title: Palestinian Is Killed After His Shooting Spree
2001-10-22.abstract: A Palestinian went on a shooting spree in a bustling commercial area in southern Jerusalem today, wounding four Israelis before he was killed by a soldier.

2001-10-22.title: Roar of the Warplanes Overhead Bolsters the Hopes of the Rebels
2001-10-22.abstract: The roar of the first jet this afternoon prompted reflexive fears of an enemy attack on this rebel-held territory. But when another and then another jet thundered overhead, flying so high they could not even be seen, it dawned on people what might finally be happening: the end of their years of isolation and defeat.     As night fell, the news spread. The Americans were starting to bomb the Taliban front lines.

2001-10-23.title: Rumsfeld Says Attacks Seek to Help Rebels Advance
2001-10-23.abstract: US warplanes concentrate strikes in Afghanistan on 16th day of bombing on front-line Taliban positions; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld says target are being hit to give direct support to anti-Taliban forces in north; says attacks on Taliban forces and Al Qaeda terrorists are intended to clear way for anti-Taliban Northern Alliance to advance; suggests attacks will continue, if necessary, through Ramadan, Muslim holy month of fasting; military officials say operations are being coordinated with rebels; photo (M)

2001-10-23.title: Hague Tribunal Chief Says Yugoslavia Still Isn't Cooperating
2001-10-23.abstract: Carla Del Ponte, chief prosecutor for UN war crimes tribunal in The Hague, accuses Yugoslav authorities of continuing to block cooperation with tribunal and of not being ready to face past; says it is not enough to turn over former leader, Slobodan Milosevic; says it must cooperate more fully to allow truth of past Balkan conflicts to emerge (M)

2001-10-23.title: Labor Party Leaders Weigh Leaving Sharon's Coalition
2001-10-23.abstract: Thousands of Israelis pour into streets of Jerusalem as Israeli forces engage in broadest action in years against Palestinians; demand that government take even tougher action; demonstration comes as leaders of Labor Party, historically nation's standardbearer of moderation and diplomatic solutions, convene to consider whether military action should drive them from Sharon's coalition (M)

2001-10-23.title: U.S. Aiming to Cut Supplies to Lift the Taliban's Grip
2001-10-23.abstract: Amid a third day of airstrikes against Taliban soldiers on the front line, Pentagon officials said for the first time that their goal was to choke off fuel, food and other supplies to the group.

2001-10-23.title: World Briefing | Asia: East Timor: Setting A Date For Independence
2001-10-23.abstract: Interim Assembly of East Timor notifies UN that it wants May 20, 2002, to be date for full independence; map (S)

2001-10-24.title: Israelis Rebuff U.S. On Pullout Request
2001-10-24.abstract: Israel turned down President Bush's request to withdraw its forces from Palestinian-controlled territory, putting new pressure on American-Israeli relations and the anti-terror coalition with Arab allies. Israel's foreign minister, Shimon Peres, said in Washington that Israel would not pull out until the Palestinians arrested the men who killed Israel's tourism minister.       Article, Page A8.

2001-10-24.title: Residents in Rebel Village Tell of Rockets Fired by Taliban Forces
2001-10-24.abstract: Residents of Charikar, Afghanistan, Northern Alliance village, say Taliban forces fired rockets at market, killing two people and wounding dozens; see move as retaliation for American airstrikes; urge continuation of bombing raids, interviews; Northern Alliances forces respond by shelling suspected Taliban position above bustling town; map; photos (M)

2001-10-24.title: Israel Rebuffs U.S. Demand To End Its West Bank Raids
2001-10-24.abstract: Israeli Foreign Minister Shimon Peres meets in Washington with Pres Bush and Sec of State Colin Powell and rebuffs their demand that Israel pull out of Palestinian-controlled areas of West Bank; says security forces of Yasir Arafat must first arrest men who murdered Israeli Tourism Minister Rehavam Zeevi; Bush and Powell balance call for immediate Israeli withdrawal with demands on Arafat for arrests in Zeeve's murder, along with suspects in terrorist attacks on Israelis; photo (M)

2001-10-24.title: New Urgency on Anthrax, Bombs and Blunders, the Ripple Effect
2001-10-24.abstract: Overview of day's events in war on terrorism and spreading anthrax scare (M)

2001-10-25.title: GROUND RAIDS SEEN AS LONG AND RISKY
2001-10-25.abstract: Adm Sir Michael Boyce, Britain's top military officer, says ground troops may have to operate in Afghanistan for weeks at a time if they are to ferret out Osama bin Laden and destroy his terrorist network; raids envisaged by Boyce could be far longer and more risky than raid carried out last week by US Special Operations forces, who were in Afghan territory for only few hours and encountered light resistance; Boyce is heavily involved in planning campaign against Taliban and bin Laden; British role is more vital now that campaign is beginning to involve ground troops; new phase of conflict plays to Britain's military strengths its commandos are among most highly regarded in world; photo (M)

2001-10-25.title: U.S. Warns Afghans That Taliban May Poison Relief Food
2001-10-25.abstract: Pentagon, citing intelligence sources, holds Taliban might poison food aid and blame United States; warns Afghans not to eat donated food from abroad if has passed through Taliban; warning is pre-emptive strike in intensifying war for public opinion in Afghanistan and broader Islamic world; Pentagon says US strikes have not wholly undercut Taliban; photo (M)

2001-10-25.title: Rebels Rely On an Elite For a Drive On Kabul
2001-10-25.abstract: Assault units Gen Ahmed Shah Massoud formed in year before his assassination, perhaps joined by Taliban defectors, could prove to be Northern Alliance's best chance to breach maze of Taliban trenches, tanks and minefields north of Kabul; units, intended to be more regular army than guerrillas, wear uniforms and have better arms than other alliance soldiers; visits to two units find soldiers who range from fierce to clumsy; photo (M)

2001-10-25.title: Amsterdam Journal; Security on the Brain, Solutions in the Eyes
2001-10-25.abstract: Amsterdam airport has one of latest technological gizmos of security conscious age: state of the art iris scanner to identify travelers; technique is part of growing field of biometric imaging, in which computers are used to identify someone's unique physical features, like palm prints, facial features and eye structure; airport planned to introduce device even before Sept 11 hijackings in United States heightened concern about air safety; photos (M)

2001-10-25.title: Israelis Storm Village in the West Bank
2001-10-25.abstract: Israeli troops storm into another West Bank village, killing at least 5 Palestinians and arresting 11 more, including two men Israelis say helped kill cabinet minister last week; Palestinian leaders accuse Israeli government of inventing link to minister's killing to cover up what they call 'massacre' of at last 10 people; raid on Palestinian town, Beit Rima, brings to seven number of villages and cities in which Israeli troops have captured positions in operation begun after killing of tourism minister, Rehavam Zeevi; latest attack comes hours after Pres Bush appeals for Israeli withdrawal from Palestinian territory; photo (M)

2001-10-26.title: World Briefing | The Americas: Brazil: Ex-Official Indicted
2001-10-26.abstract: Paulo Maluf, former presidential candidate in Brazil and Sao Paulo mayor, is charged with not declaring campaign donations in 1992 and 1998 (S)

2001-10-26.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ukraine: Land Reform Roils Communists
2001-10-26.abstract: Communist legislators stormed the podium and unfurled banners in Parliament in a protest aimed at stopping a proposal that would allow the state to sell its vast farmland holdings to private buyers. The 450-member Parliament has 113 Communists, and the Interfax news agency reported that nearly all of them took part in the protest, holding banners that read ''To sell land is to sell the homeland.'' Ukraine, once a Soviet breadbasket, has 81.5 million acres of fertile farmland but efforts to overhaul the Soviet collective-farm system have gone slowly and production is low.     Michael Wines (NYT)

2001-10-26.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: Anthrax Scare On Tokyo Subway
2001-10-26.abstract: One of Tokyo's main subway liÿ

2001-10-26.title: World Briefing | Africa: Nigeria: President Reins In Soldiers
2001-10-26.abstract: Nigerian Pres Olusegun Obasanjo orders soldiers to sto

2001-10-27.title: Swiss Lower Estimate of Toll in Tunnel Fire
2001-10-27.abstract: Swiss police say crash and subsequent fire in St Gotthard Tunnel apparently killed far fewer people than first feared; say they have confirmed 11 deaths, with 30 travelers still missing; fire exposes ventilation system weaknesses in heavily traveled tunnel, thought to be one of safest in Europe; map; photo (M)

2001-10-27.title: U.S. Is Planning an Aid Package for Pakistan That Is Worth Billions
2001-10-27.abstract: Bush administration has put together aid package for Pakistan that is likely to total several billion dollars and includes sweeping debt rescheduling, grants stretching over many years and trade benefits as reward for its support against terrorism; package, which is encountering some resistance in Washington and abroad, would represent departure from often glacial process of aiding poor countries in recent years; while support may reward Pres Pervez Musharraf for his backing of American-led coalition, critics worry that politically inspired aid could be misdirected; some American lawmakers say Bush administration may have too readily agreed to give Pakistan about $600 million in cash this year and next without reliable way of ensuring that money would be used to improve health and education rather than to underwrite military or Islamic militants that Pakistan backs in disputed territory of Kashmir; Rep Jim McDermott has reservations about aid, while Rep Jim Kolbe opposes it (M)

2001-10-27.title: Egypt's Elite Torn by Arab Roots and Western Ties
2001-10-27.abstract: Egypt's Westernized elite, used to effortlessly melding Western and Arab cultures, anxiously seek neutral ground as United States war on terrorism in Afghanistan is widely seen in Egypt as Western war against Arabs and Islam; are uncomfortable with virulent anti-Americanism that has become part of normal public discourse but feel unable to intervene in face of open attacks on US in government-controlled press; photo (M)

2001-10-27.title: Talks Fail to Entice Montenegro to Stay Yugoslav
2001-10-27.abstract: Talks between Yugoslav Pres Vojislav Kostunica and Montenegrin Pres Milo Djukanovic break off without accord on how to run federal Yugoslav state; both leaders say Montenegro must hold independence referendum in spring (M)

2001-10-28.title: Concern Grows Over Fate of Another Foe of the Taliban
2001-10-28.abstract: Anxiety has increased about safety of Hamid Qarzai, potential post-Taliban leader, who is believed to have traveled to Afghanistan to incite uprising among his tribesmen; Qarzai, who is favored by some Washington strategists as best bet for leading alliance of southern Afghans against Taliban, is reported by his associates to be in countryside around Kandahar, Taliban capital and home of his tribe; Quetta, Pakistan, has been his base (M)

2001-10-28.title: BUSH ADVISER SAYS RUSSIA IS WARMING TO U.S. ABM TESTS
South African White Party, Hurt by Feud, Collapses
2001-10-28.abstract: South Africa's leading white opposition party, Democratic Alliance, collapses amid internal feud over leadership of Tony Leon; had sought to build support among blacks; African National Congress, in move seemingly aimed at spurring further defections from opposition, says it will change laws that bar sitting members of Parliament from switching parties (M)a

2001-10-28.title: Fire Apparently Spread In Sunken Russian Sub
Panama Is Putting Journalists on Trial
2001-10-28.abstract: Over 70 journalists face criminal charges in Panama, whose draconian press laws lets public officials bring 'disrespect charges' against journalists who publish exposes or unflattering commentaries; most defendants in cases already settled have been pardoned or fined; laws also let officials block press access to wide variety of government documents deemed confidential almost by whim; journalists charge laws are part of antidemocratic effort to silence and punish critics; cartoon (M)/

2001-10-29.title: 'Peace Is Possible,' Basque Separatist Group Says
2001-10-29.abstract: Spain's Basque separatists say they want peace, but only if Spanish and French allow vote on independence in region; want referendum that includes French Basque lands and Spanish province of Navarra (S)

2001-10-29.title: China's Heir Apparent in Debut on the World Stage
2001-10-29.abstract: Article profiles China's vice president Hu Jintao, heir apparent to aging Jiang Zemin, who is making first visit to Russia and Western Europe in common diplomatic trip filled with meaning; it is seen as another calibrated step in Hu's cautious but apperently effective ascension, one that under peculiarties of Chinese politics requires him to keep head down; photos (M)

2001-10-29.title: Rio Journal; The Singer, the Scandal, The Jailhouse Pregnancy
2001-10-29.abstract: Article on scandal involving Mexican pop star Gloria Trevi and manager Sergio Andrade, both of whom fled to Brazil to avoid prosecution for sexually abusing teenage girls and are awaiting extradition; Trevi who says her life would be endangered if she returns to Mexico, has been imprisoned since February 2000; has become pregnant in jail in apparent attempt to take advantage of provision in Brazilian law that prevents extradition of foreigners who are parents of Brazilian child; photos (M)

2001-10-29.title: The French Seem a Bit Friendlier
2001-10-29.abstract: Gabrielle Parnes, American who lives in Paris, talks about living abroad in wake of Sept 11 terrorist attacks on US; photo (S)

2001-10-29.title: Slain Taliban Foe Is Buried. So They Say.
2001-10-29.abstract: Modest funeral without a body is held for Abdul Haq, former Afghan guerrilla commander who was killed by Taliban as he traveled eastern Afghanistan on secretive mission to incite uprising; service is held in Peshawar, Pakistan; family spokesman says Taliban would not allow body to be brought across border to Pakistan, but Haq's brother, Haji din Muhammad, blames Pakistani 'political agent' who refused to allow body through checkpoint; Pakistan's intelligence agency has frequently been accused of backing Haq's plans to topple Taliban, and now its agents are being accused of betraying Haq in ugly double deal; facts about Haq's last journey into Afghanistan are murky; Taliban claim that one or more 'Americans' were with Haq and narrowly escaped; former national security adviser, Robert C McFarlane, says United States did try last-minute assistance, but it proved too late for Haq; photos (M)

2001-10-30.title: Taliban Continue Trade Through Closed but Porous Border
2001-10-30.abstract: Border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is officially closed, but it is so porous that wounded Taliban fighters cross for treatment and easy flow of commerce and people between two countries continues, helping Afghanistan retain semblance of normalcy; Taliban profits, taxing shipments into Pakistan; closed border was condition set by Washington when it established relationship with Pakistan, but fabled, centuries-old smuggling route has not changed much and Pashtun tribesmen on both sides of border feel more allegiance among themselves than to respective countries; photo (M)

2001-10-30.title: Israeli Troops in No Hurry To Leave West Bank Towns
2001-10-30.abstract: Israeli forces remain in Palestinian enclaves of Tulkarm, Jenin, Qalqilya and Ramallah in West Bank after vacating Bethlehem and Beit Jala; has remained entrenched, despite American pleas to leave immediately; Israeli government says soldiers are vital bulwark against terrorist attacks; photo (M)

2001-10-30.title: World Briefing | Asia: India-Pakistan: No Talks At U.N.
2001-10-30.abstract: Indian Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee, after meeting in Delhi with German Chancellor Gerhard Schroder, rejects proposal to meet with Pakistani Gen Pervez Musharraf in New York during United Nations General Assembly (S)

2001-10-30.title: Funeral Held For Christians In Pakistan
2001-10-30.abstract: Thousands of mourners in Behawalpur, Pakistan, bewail killings of 15 Christians at funeral procession for victims of shootings at St Dominic's Roman Catholic Church; no group has claimed responsibility for attack, however, Muslim militants are under suspicion (M)

2001-10-31.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Tribute To Turkish Workers
2001-10-31.abstract: Chancellor Gerhard Schroder lauds economic contribution of Turkish immigrants as Germany marks 40th anniversary of accord inviting Turkish workers to Germany (M)

2001-10-31.title: U.S. Campaign On 2nd Front: Public Opinion
2001-10-31.abstract: Defense Sec Donald Rumsfeld will leave this week for Russia and nations near Afghanistan as Bush administration strives to hold together coalition for military campaign against Osama bin Laden and Taliban; Gen Tommy Franks meets with Uzbek leaders, saying US military activity may change from day to day; Sen Carl Levin says in interview that US may have to add to special operations forces to intensify pressure; British Sec Geoff Hoon, in Washington, says allies should make clear that they are 'taking account of Ramadan' holy month to avoid further strains with Muslim countries; photos; Rumsfeld observes that Taliban and Al Qaeda are 'unlikely to take a holiday' from trying to kill Americans; Prime Min Tony Blair tells Britons not to forget horror of Sept 11 attacks (M)

2001-10-31.title: Britain Charges Man in Death Of a Top Afghan Rebel Leader
2001-10-31.abstract: Yasser al-Siri, Egyptian who gave letter of accreditation to two men who posed as television journalists to assassinate Afghan rebel leader Ahmed Shah Massoud, is charged with conspiracy under Britain's antiterrorism law; photo; Siri sought asylum in 1994 after Egyptian court convicted him in absentia in failed bomb attack against prime minister that killed schoolgirl (S)

2001-10-31.title: Flood of Asylum Seekers Closes Channel Tunnel for Freight
2001-10-31.abstract: Channel Tunnel is closed to freight traffic for 11 hours after dozens of asylum seekers break into freight yard near Calais, France, and try to hide on trains bound for Britain; some 150 migrants are arrested (M)

2001-10-31.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ukraine: Stock Market Monitor Slain
2001-10-31.abstract: Olexy Romashko, Ukrainian official who policed legality of stock market transactions, is found murdered in his apartment building elevator, apparent victim of mafia-connected clans that dominate nation's business and financial sectors (S)
